title: Wood Step Stool with Splayed Legs
summary: A single-step stool with legs beveled outward for a wider, more tip-resistant stance — the same size and material system as this catalog's straight-leg version, with one angle changed.
description:
This stool is nearly identical in size and material to this catalog's straight-leg step stool, with exactly one change: the legs are beveled at 5° instead of cut square, splaying the base outward slightly for a wider, more stable footprint. That single-angle bevel (not a double compound cut) keeps the joinery simple while still giving the stool real outward flare.

Cut the two 1x10 leg pieces with their bevel parallel to each other, then cut a triangular notch out of the bottom of each — a hand-hold detail that also lightens the look of an otherwise solid leg panel. Mark 1/2" in from the top of each leg and draw a reference line before assembly; that mark is what keeps the apron pieces positioned consistently on both legs.

The two apron pieces are cut as a trapezoid rather than a rectangle, both ends at a 5° angle with no bevel, so they sit flush against the splayed legs without a gap. Attach the top last, straight through the pre-drilled pocket holes — the same final step as the straight-leg version, just landing on a slightly wider base.

tools.note: The 5° leg and apron bevel/angle cuts.

cutList.note: Both ends beveled at 5°, long point to short point, ends parallel; triangular notch jigsawed out of the bottom.
Short point to short point, both ends cut at a 5° angle (no bevel), ends not parallel — a trapezoid shape.

steps.title: Cut and notch the legs
steps.body: Set a miter saw to a 5° bevel and cut the two leg pieces to a long-point-to-short-point measurement of 7-1/2", ends parallel. Cut a triangular hand-hold notch out of the bottom of each leg with a jigsaw. Mark 1/2" in from the top of each leg and draw a reference line for the apron position.

steps.title: Cut the aprons
steps.body: Set the miter saw to a 5° angle with no bevel and cut the two apron pieces to a short-point-to-short-point measurement of 13", ends not parallel. Drill 3/4" pocket holes on the ends.

steps.title: Assemble the base
steps.body: Attach the aprons to the legs using the pre-drilled pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and glue, lining up with your reference marks.

steps.title: Attach the top and finish
steps.body: Attach the top to the base through the predrilled pocket holes with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and glue. Add knobs if desired. Sand and finish.

unresolvedImages.alt: Small step stool with outward-splayed beveled legs
